items.title: Clarendon | University of Oxford
items.funding: Clarendon scholarship cover course fees and provide a grant for living expenses for the period of fee liability of a scholar's course.
requirements: Clarendon Scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic excellence and potential across all graduate degree-bearing subjects at the University of Oxford.
All full-time and part-time DPhil and Master’s courses are eligible.
items.eligibility: There are no restrictions on nationality, ordinary residence or field of study.
items.summary: Clarendon not only offers over 200 new, fully-funded scholarships each year to assist outstanding graduate scholars, but also the opportunity to join one of the most active, internationally diverse, and multidisciplinary communities at Oxford. In 2025/26 Clarendon is celebrating its 25th intake of exceptional scholars.

items.title: Knight-Hennessy Scholars Funding
items.funding: A fellowship applied directly to cover tuition and associated fees Financial Support for Cost of Attendance The Medical School will fund the remaining years, consistent with its standard funding commitment to MD students, which consists of tuition support and stipend.
requirements: Knight-Hennessy scholars receive funding for up to three years for any graduate degree(s) at Stanford.
This funding applies to the degree(s) to which scholars are preparing to enroll at the time of scholar selection.

If a scholar is admitted to a degree program that exceeds three years — such as an MD, PhD, or Stanford dual- or joint-degree program — then funding is based on the graduate degree program’s standard funding commitment.
items.eligibility: Fellowship stipends are taxable, but not subject to withholding (except for international students from a country without a tax treaty).
items.summary: Scholars receive funding for up to three years for any graduate degree(s) at Stanford, applicable to the degree(s) they plan to enroll in at the time of selection.

items.title: Postgraduate Cambridge University Scholarship | Gates Cambridge
items.funding: A fully funded graduate education for future leaders committed to improving the lives of others..
requirements: Each year Gates Cambridge offers c.80 full-cost scholarships to outstanding applicants from countries outside the UK to pursue a postgraduate degree in any subject available at the University of Cambridge.
You should let the Trust know ASAP if you are awarded any funding towards your degree at Cambridge.

criteria: All students including international students University of Geneva Masters (MS) Degree Master of Science degree in one of the disciplines offered by the Faculty of Sciences For the application to be considered, each student has to fulfill the enrollment procedure as described on the Admissions office website.

items.summary: 2026/27 University of Geneva Excellence Masters Scholarships are open to all students around the world. The scholarships are towards Masters Programmes under the Faculty of Science at University of Geneva in Switzerland. Deadline is 28 February 2026. Courses start September 2026. Read more »

items.title: Government of Ireland International Education Scholarships | Policy | Higher Education Authority
items.benefits: Benefits found, but the exact tuition and stipend package still needs review.
items.funding: Benefits found, but the exact tuition and stipend package still needs review.
requirements: Applicants must have a conditional or final offer of admission to an eligible Irish higher education institution at the time of applying for a Government of Ireland International Education Scholarship.
Proof of admission must be submitted with the GOI-IES application, in the relevant section of the GOI-IES application form.
Prospective candidates who have previously held a GOI-IES scholarship are not eligible to apply.
criteria: The Government of Ireland International Education Scholarship (GOI-IES) programme supports high-calibre international students who wish to study at NFQ levels 9 or 10 (master’s, postgraduate diploma or PhD) in Ireland [1].

Applicants must have a conditional or final offer of admission to an eligible Irish higher education institution at the time of applying for a Government of Ireland International Education Scholarship.
Proof of admission must be submitted with the GOI-IES application, in the relevant section of the GOI-IES application form.
Prospective candidates who have previously held a GOI-IES scholarship are not eligible to apply.
items.eligibility: The Government of Ireland International Education Scholarship (GOI-IES) programme supports high-calibre international students who wish to study at NFQ levels 9 or 10 (master’s, postgraduate diploma or PhD) in Ireland [1].
